name: CascadeEval
alternateName: CascadeEval: Stage-Level Evaluation for Multi-Stage LLM Defense Pipelines
description: CascadeEval is a staged evaluation artifact for Input Guard -> Target LLM -> Output Guard defense pipelines. The staging snapshot contains 7,010 jailbreak prompts, 275 complete defense configurations, stage-level logs, a stage-level Knowledge Base, C3A adaptive stress-test outputs, and human-evaluation calibration materials. Raw harmful prompt data should be released through gated or reviewer-accessible channels.
citeAs: Anonymous authors. CascadeEval: Stage-Level Evaluation for Multi-Stage LLM Defense Pipelines. NeurIPS Evaluations and Datasets submission artifact, 2026.
citation: Anonymous authors. CascadeEval: Stage-Level Evaluation for Multi-Stage LLM Defense Pipelines. NeurIPS Evaluations and Datasets submission artifact, 2026.
license: CC BY 4.0 for documentation, metadata, aggregate statistics, and derived tables; MIT for CascadeEval-authored scripts; Custom Gated Research-Use Terms for raw harmful prompts, model responses, adaptive traces, and KB retrieval exemplars.

recordSet.name: stage_level_evaluations
recordSet.description: Prompt-level stage records for 7,010 jailbreak prompts and evaluated defense components.

field.name: prompt_text_en
field.description: Jailbreak prompt text. Contains harmful content and should be gated.

recordSet.name: c3a_jbb_goals
recordSet.description: The 100 JailbreakBench JBB-Behaviors goals used for C3A online evaluation. Contains harmful goal text and is gated.

recordSet.name: c3a_results
recordSet.description: Paper-ready C3A result tables and confidence intervals.

recordSet.name: c3a_stage_distribution
recordSet.description: Stage-distribution diagnostics for Config A/B/C C3A runs.

recordSet.name: c3a_guidance_distribution
recordSet.description: Guidance-type diagnostics for Config A/B/C C3A runs.

rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal information is intentionally collected as a dataset field. Free-form prompts, responses, and annotator comments may contain incidental personal, credential-like, or operational strings; raw files are therefore gated and subject to takedown review.
rai:dataBiases: The prompt corpus is derived from atmaCup #21 materials and subsequent English normalization, deduplication, and taxonomy labeling. It reflects competition incentives, prompt-battle behavior, selected harmfulness categories, and the evaluated guard/model grid rather than all real-world misuse patterns.
rai:annotationsPerItem: Human calibration uses a stratified 240-sample set with multiple independent annotations per item and an aggregate human-majority label. Public releases should expose anonymized or aggregate labels only.

rai:intendedUseCase: Authorized research on multi-stage LLM safety evaluation.
Stage-level attribution of Input Guard, Target LLM, and Output Guard behavior.
Reproduction of CascadeEval paper tables, figures, and confidence intervals.
Defensive analysis of bottleneck-dependent adaptive stress testing.
rai:discouragedUseCase: Training or fine-tuning systems for harmful generation.
Building operational jailbreak tooling or prompt libraries.
Redistributing raw harmful prompts, model responses, adaptive traces, or KB exemplars outside the gated process.

rai:limitations: Contains harmful jailbreak prompts and model outputs. Does not constitute a deployment-safety certification. Raw harmful data should be gated.
rai:misuseRisk: Raw prompts, model responses, successful adaptive traces, and KB exemplars could be misused to improve jailbreak attempts. The recommended release mode is gated or reviewer-accessible distribution.
rai:dataCollection: The artifact aggregates jailbreak prompts, stage-level defense outcomes, C3A adaptive stress-test logs, and human-evaluation calibration summaries collected for CascadeEval. The release uses an internal IRB-equivalent safeguard review for harmful-content research artifacts: gated raw access, misuse restrictions, anonymized human-evaluation outputs, and a reporting path for takedown or harmful-content concerns. Human annotators were engaged for harmfulness calibration under an annotation protocol with content warning, blinding to configuration metadata, and anonymized public identifiers. Competition-source prompt consent and redistribution are governed by atmaCup #21/platform rules. The authors have obtained permission to include atmaCup #21-derived prompt data in the CascadeEval submission artifact under gated or reviewer-accessible research access; raw prompt text remains gated and is not treated as unrestricted public data.
